column1: # Cross-border transmission reference data — Black Sea region
# mw_fwd = capacity in from_country → to_country direction
# mw_rev = capacity in to_country → from_country direction
# status: existing | under_construction | planned | candidate | long_term | cold_standby

column13: note
Two parallel 400 kV circuits. NTC asymmetric: ~650 MW BGR→TUR / ~500 MW TUR→BGR (SIPS-limited). 1350 MW STC. SIPS at Hamitabat.
Single 400 kV circuit. SIPS will trip Turkey if sole connection to CESA exceeds threshold.
2nd Greece–Turkey 400 kV line (~70 km in GRC). PCI project / East Balkan Corridor.
Proposed Med-TSO Project #11. Would raise total CESA↔TR NTC combined with 2nd GR-TR line. TBD capacity.
Akhaltsikhe substation: 2×350 MW B2B converters (Siemens). 500 kV line into TR via Borçka. Commissioned 2013.
3rd B2B converter unit at Akhaltsikhe + new 400 kV line Akhaltsikhe–Tortum. Brings TUR-GEO NTC to ~1050 MW.
Achara line. Mostly reserve/out of service. Planned upgrade with 350 MW B2B at Batumi.
Mukhrani Valley line. Crosses border near Red Bridge. GEO→AZE: 1300 MW / AZE→GEO: 1500 MW. Commissioned 2016.
Gardabani double-circuit line. Reconstructed 2019–2020 (124 of 162 towers by Sakrusenergo).
Kavkasioni line. Parallel synchronous operation. GEO→RUS: 570 MW / RUS→GEO: 650 MW.
Salkhino line. Operated in isolated mode mostly. GEO→RUS: 264 MW / RUS→GEO: 300 MW.
Nakaduli line. Low-voltage isolated. GEO→RUS: 40 MW / RUS→GEO: 45 MW.
Alaverdi line. Operated in isolated mode mostly. GEO→ARM: 150 MW / ARM→GEO: 100 MW. New HVDC under construction in parallel.
Caucasus Transmission Network. EIB/EU/KfW funded. Three B2B units planned (3×350 MW = 1050 MW total).
Synchronous link Armenia–Iran (Iran grid). AGIR project would upgrade and add HVDC parallel.
Khoy–Van 400 kV B2B HVDC, 600 MW. TEIAS + Iran Grid Mgmt Co. operations contract signed Jan 2024.
Operational. Southeast Türkiye to Iraqi Kurdistan grid.
Originally fed 2 Birecik HPP units to Syria pre-2010. Disconnected when TEIAS synced to ENTSO-E. B2B planned if reconnected.
Junction SS Yashma (renovated 2022, 750 MW capacity). Strategic IPS interconnection since 1971.
Parallel link to Yashma–Derbent.
Low-voltage cross-border supply.
Second 330 kV line to double exchange capacity with Russia.
Main of 5 PTLs Azerbaijan–Iran. Total combined NTC ~600 MW.
260 towers, ~68 km. Reconstructed 2019.
One of 5 operational PTLs Azerbaijan–Iran.
Connection from Araz HPP / Khudafarin HPP region.
Re-sectioned from former Stupina–Varna line in 2024.
Re-sectioned from former Rahman–Dobrudja line in 2024. Capacity boost: +200–300 MW export, +100 MW import.
Western Romania–Bulgaria link via the Danube. Total RO-BG NTC ~1500 MW.
Iron Gates HPP cross-border. Long-standing link.
63 km in Romania. Commissioned January 2025. Brings total RO-SR capacity to 1000 MW.
Double-circuit upgrade. ENTSO-E TYNDP project of mutual interest.
Long-standing 400 kV link.
Double-circuit border reach 1 GW. Commissioned with Oradea Sud–Nădab 2020.
Second ROU-HUN interconnection planned.
Main RO-UA interconnection. Part of EE CCR. CESA since 2022.
Goes via Moldova (Vulcănești) onward to Ukraine. Damaged by drone strike 03/2026.
Pre-existing 400 kV line. Key Moldova energy security link.
Phase I Moldova–Romania. 157 km new VL-CH. 630 MVA nominal. Commissioning end 2025.
Phase II Moldova–Romania interconnection.
First BG-GR interconnection (legacy).
Second BG-GR interconnection. PCI project. 2 GW nominal. July 2023. Asymmetric: 1400 MW BG→GR / 1700 MW GR→BG.
BG-RS interconnection.
BG-MK interconnection.
Nakhchivan–Iran lines. Nakhchivan isolated from AZE main grid. ~33 GWh/yr exchanged.
Nakhchivan–Turkey. Only AZE-TUR land border link. Low capacity.
Nakhchivan reconnection to AZE main grid via Turkey. Azerbaijan's first 400 kV converter. ~230 km.
Zangelan route. 74 km main + 105 km Nakh section + 44 km corridor. Will reconnect Nakhchivan synchronously to AZE main grid.
Alternative transit via Iranian territory. Announced Apr 2025.
